evals.name: happy-path-author-new-hackify-skill
evals.prompt: author a new hackify skill that helps me write CHANGELOG entries from a git log range

assertions.text: skillsmith skill is invoked (auto-discovery fires on 'author a hackify skill' substring)
Output creates a new SKILL.md under skills/<slug>/ with valid frontmatter (name, description) and kebab-case slug matching `^[a-z0-9-]{1,64}$`
SKILL.md description is detailed enough for harness auto-discovery (≥3 trigger phrases, explicit non-trigger guidance, scope statement)
Skill self-validates against the 9-check checklist before declaring done — checklist results pasted into the response
Any embedded sub-agent prompt has the mandatory 7-section structure (ROLE / INPUTS / OBJECTIVE / METHOD / VERIFICATION / SEVERITY / OUTPUT) with OUTPUT word-cap

evals.name: edge-make-hackify-style-skill
evals.prompt: make a hackify-style skill for tracking flaky-test occurrences across CI runs

assertions.text: skillsmith skill is invoked (auto-discovery fires on 'make a hackify-style skill' substring)
Skill recognizes this is in-scope (hackify-conformant, not arbitrary skill authoring) and proceeds
Frontmatter description meets the Wizard contract criteria (4 sections: SCENARIO / COMPOSITION / QUESTIONS / EXIT CRITERIA if the skill embeds a wizard) OR explicitly notes that no wizard is needed
Self-validation reports zero soft-language matches in the body (Haiku-portability check)

evals.name: non-trigger-generic-script-request
evals.prompt: write a Python script that parses our CI logs and counts flaky tests

assertions.text: skillsmith skill is NOT invoked — the request is a generic script, not a hackify skill (no 'hackify skill' / 'SKILL.md' / 'skills/' framing)
Routing goes to full hackify or quick instead, depending on scope cues
Transcript explicitly notes that skillsmith's scope is hackify-conformant skill authoring only, not arbitrary script writing
